sections.label: Section 733
sections.translation: Have you got anything ?

sections.label: Section 733b
sections.translation: No ; by Jupiter , certainly not !

sections.label: Section 733c
sections.translation: Nothing at all ?

sections.label: Section 734
sections.original: οὐδέν γε πλὴν ἢ τὸ πέος ἐν τῇ δεξιᾷ .
sections.translation: Nothing , except what I have in my right hand .

sections.label: Section 735
sections.translation: Will you not quickly cover yourself up and think of something ?

sections.label: Section 736
sections.original: περὶ τοῦ ; σὺ γάρ μοι τοῦτο φράσον ὦ Σώκρατες .
sections.translation: About what ? For do you tell me this , O Socrates !

sections.label: Section 737
sections.original: αὐτὸς ὅ τι βούλει πρῶτος ἐξευρὼν λέγε .
sections.translation: Do you , yourself , first find out and state what you wish .

sections.label: Section 738
sections.translation: You have heard a thousand times what I wish . About the interest ; so that I may pay no one .

sections.label: Section 740
sections.original: ἴθι νῦν καλύπτου καὶ σχάσας τὴν φροντίδα
sections.translation: Come then , wrap yourself up , and having given your mind play with subtilty , revolve your affairs by little and little , rightly distinguishing and examining .

sections.label: Section 742b
sections.translation: Ah me , unhappy man !

sections.label: Section 743
sections.original: ἔχʼ ἀτρέμα · κἂν ἀπορῇς τι τῶν νοημάτων ,
sections.translation: Keep quiet ; and if you be puzzled in any one of your conceptions , leave it and go ; and then set your mind in motion again , and lock it up .

sections.label: Section 746
sections.translation: ( in great glee ). O dearest little Socrates !

sections.label: Section 746b
sections.translation: What , old man ?

sections.label: Section 747
sections.translation: I have got a device for cheating them of the interest .

sections.label: Section 748
sections.translation: Exhibit it .

sections.label: Section 748b
sections.translation: [*] Now tell me this —

sections.label: Section 748c
sections.translation: Pray ?

sections.label: Section 749
sections.translation: If I were to purchase a Thessalian witch ,

sections.label: Section 750
sections.original: καθέλοιμι νύκτωρ τὴν σελήνην , εἶτα δὴ
sections.translation: and draw down the moon by night , and then shut it up , as if it were a mirror , in a round crest-case , and then carefully keep it —

sections.label: Section 753
sections.original: τί δῆτα τοῦτʼ ἂν ὠφελήσειέν σʼ ;
sections.translation: What good , pray , would this do you ?

sections.label: Section 753b
sections.translation: What ? If the moon were to rise no longer anywhere , I should not pay the interest .

sections.label: Section 755b
sections.translation: Why so , pray ?

sections.label: Section 756
sections.translation: Because the money is lent out by the month .

sections.label: Section 757
sections.original: εὖ γʼ · ἀλλʼ ἕτερον αὖ σοι προβαλῶ τι δεξιόν .
sections.translation: Capital ! But I will again propose to you another clever question . If a suit of five talents should be entered against you , tell me how you would obliterate it .

sections.label: Section 761
sections.original: ὅπως ; ὅπως ; οὐκ οἶδʼ · ἀτὰρ ζητητέον .
sections.translation: How ? How ? I do not know but I must seek .

sections.label: Section 762
sections.original: μή νυν περὶ σαυτὸν εἶλλε τὴν γνώμην ἀεί ,
sections.translation: Do not then always revolve your thoughts about yourself ; but slack away your mind into the air , like a cock-chafer tied with a thread by the foot .

sections.label: Section 765
sections.translation: I have found a very clever method of getting rid of my suit , so that you yourself would acknowledge it .

sections.label: Section 766b
sections.translation: Of what description ?

sections.label: Section 767
sections.original: ἤδη παρὰ τοῖσι φαρμακοπώλαις τὴν λίθον
sections.translation: Have you ever seen this stone in the chemist ’ s shops , the beautiful and transparent one , from which they kindle fire ?

sections.label: Section 769b
sections.translation: Do you mean the burning-glass ?

sections.label: Section 770
sections.original: ἔγωγε . φέρε τί δῆτʼ ἄν , εἰ ταύτην λαβών ,
sections.translation: I do . Come what would you say , pray , if I were to take this , when the clerk was entering the suit , and were to stand at a distance , in the direction of the sun , thus , and melt out the letters of my suit ?

sections.label: Section 773
sections.translation: Cleverly done , by the Graces !

sections.label: Section 773b
sections.translation: Oh ! How I am delighted , that a suit of five talents has been cancelled !

sections.label: Section 775
sections.translation: Come now , quickly seize upon this .

sections.label: Section 775b
sections.translation: What ?

sections.label: Section 776
sections.translation: How , when engaged in a lawsuit , you could overturn the suit , when you were about to be cast , because you had no witnesses .

sections.label: Section 778
sections.translation: Most readily and easily .

sections.label: Section 778b
sections.translation: Tell me , pray .

sections.label: Section 778c
sections.translation: Well now , I ’ ll tell you . If , while one suit was still pending , before mine was called on , I were to run away and hang myself .

sections.label: Section 781
sections.translation: You talk nonsense .

sections.label: Section 781b
sections.translation: By the gods , would I ! For no one will bring action against me when I am dead .

sections.label: Section 783
sections.original: ὑθλεῖς · ἄπερρʼ , οὐκ ἂν διδάξαιμʼ ἄν σʼ ἔτι .
sections.translation: You talk nonsense . Begone ; I can ’ t teach you any longer .

sections.label: Section 784
sections.original: ὁτιὴ τί ; ναὶ πρὸς τῶν θεῶν ὦ Σώκρατες .
sections.translation: Why so ? Yea , by the gods , O Socrates !

sections.label: Section 785
sections.original: ἀλλʼ εὐθὺς ἐπιλήθει σύ γʼ ἅττʼ ἂν καὶ μάθῃς ·
sections.translation: You straightaway forget whatever you learn . For what now was the first thing you were taught ? Tell me .

sections.label: Section 787
sections.original: φέρʼ ἴδω τί μέντοι πρῶτον ἦν ; τί πρῶτον ἦν ;
sections.translation: Come , let me see : nay , what was the first ? What was the fist ? Nay , what was the thing in which we knead our flour ? Ah me ! What was it ?

sections.label: Section 789b
sections.translation: Will you not pack off to the devil , you most forgetful and most stupid old man ?

sections.label: Section 791
sections.original: οἴμοι τί οὖν δῆθʼ ὁ κακοδαίμων πείσομαι ;
sections.translation: Ah me , what then , pray will become of me , wretched man ? For I shall be utterly undone , if I do not learn to ply the tongue . Come , O ye Clouds , give me some good advice .

sections.label: Section 794
sections.translation: We , old man , advise you , if you have a son grown up , to send him to learn in your stead .

sections.label: Section 797
sections.original: ἀλλʼ ἔστʼ ἔμοιγʼ υἱὸς καλός τε κἀγαθός ·
sections.translation: Well , I have a fine , handsome son , but he is not willing to learn . What must I do ?

sections.label: Section 799
sections.translation: But do you permit him ?

sections.label: Section 799b
sections.translation: Yes , for he is robust in body , and in good health , and is come of the high-plumed dames of Coesyra . I will go for him , and if he be not willing , I will certainly drive him from my house . To Socrates . Go in and wait for me a short time .

sections.label: Section 804
sections.original: ἆρʼ αἰσθάνει πλεῖστα διʼ ἡμᾶς ἀγάθʼ αὐτίχʼ ἕξων
sections.translation: Do you perceive that you are soon to obtain the greatest benefits through us alone of the gods ? For this man is ready to do everything that you bid him .

sections.label: Section 810
sections.original: σὺ δʼ ἀνδρὸς ἐκπεπληγμένου καὶ φανερῶς ἐπηρμένου
sections.translation: But you , while the man is astounded and evidently elated , having perceived it , will quickly fleece him to the best of your power . Exit Socrates . For matters of this sort are somehow accustomed to turn the other way .

sections.label: Section 814
sections.original: οὔτοι μὰ τὴν Ὁμίχλην ἔτʼ ἐνταυθοῖ μενεῖς ·
sections.translation: By Mist , you certainly shall not stay here any longer ! But go and gnaw the columns of Megacles .

sections.label: Section 816
sections.original: ὦ δαιμόνιε , τί χρῆμα πάσχεις ὦ πάτερ ;
sections.translation: My good sir , what is the matter with you , O father ? You are not in your senses , by Olympian Jupiter !

sections.label: Section 818
sections.original: ἰδού γʼ ἰδού , Δίʼ Ὀλύμπιον · τῆς μωρίας ,
sections.translation: See , see , Olympian Jupiter ! What folly ! To think of your believing in Jupiter , as old as you are !

sections.label: Section 820
sections.translation: Why , pray , did you laugh at this ?

sections.label: Section 820b
sections.translation: Reflecting that you are a child , and have antiquated notions . Yet , however , approach , that you may know more ; and I will tell you a thing , by learning which you will be a man . But see that you do not teach this to any one .

sections.label: Section 825
sections.translation: Well , what is it ?

sections.label: Section 825b
sections.translation: You swore now by Jupiter .
